ref: News about this spread all over that part of the country. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men
text: Ki bi din mɔɔnt li labaar tingbouŋ maŋ ni kur po. Yisa nba din nyɔɔnt jɔɔnii bannlee ninbina biaŋinba
translated: "The man who was speaking in the language of the Tingboung people could not understand the language of the Bannlee people."

ref: He even had to tell his disciples to get a boat ready to keep him from being crushed by the crowds.
text: Ki u bet u poorpoweiteeb a bin la burgbetir turɔ a wun kɔɔ ki kar ki wann niib maŋ Yennu maan, a ki nibur na tan daa tutɔ,
translated: "In the past, there was a rich man who lived in a big castle with many wives and children, and he had a large farm where he grew crops and kept livestock."

ref: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
text: Tiŋaŋ kan lon lɔɔnbiiti; tibiiuk mun bia kan lon lɔɔnŋana.
translated: The old man is selling yams; the woman is buying yams from the old man.

ref: When Jesus was eating with his twelve disciples that evening, he said, “One of you will surely hand me over to my enemies.”
text: Ba din di yoonba ki Yisa yetib a “Barmɔniie ki n beeri na, i ni yenɔ sa nyi n poor ki fikisin.”
translated: "The man named Yisa said, 'Barmoniie, come here quickly, for we are about to face a great danger.'"

ref: If David called the Messiah his Lord, how can the Messiah be a son of King David?”
text: Li paak, David nba din yiin Masia a ‘Yomdaanɔ,’ ki nlee ki Masia tee ŋɔɔ David yaaboonni?”
translated: "Who is David playing with in the team named 'Yomdaanɔ,' is it Masia who is playing with him?"

ref: “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
text: “N beerae, fiit, ki jii a liaŋ kii kun a ŋaak ni.”
translated: "In the forest, there is a man who always wears a red hat."

ref: But Peter followed along at a distance and came to the courtyard of the high priest's palace. He went in and sat down with the guards to see what was going to happen.
text: Ba saan nan Yisa na, ki Piita waa u poorpo banfɔkira, ki saa baar see Yennu jiantu yudaanɔ maŋ ŋaak nanyer po, ki tan kɔɔ dundɔuŋ ni, ki saan saa kar toontunna boor ni, a wun la li joontu.
translated: The man Yisa, who is Piita, has a big farm, which is located far from the town Yennu, where there is a dense forest, and a river flows nearby, and there is a large house there, and a small hut.

ref: and when I was naked, you gave me clothes to wear. When I was sick, you took care of me, and when I was in jail, you visited me.”
text: N din somm yanne ki i gbabinin; ki n din yiar ki i sommin; ki n din kɔɔ dansarik, ki i yɔɔ gɔntin.’
translated: "I will not dance with a woman who does not know how to walk; who does not know how to talk; who does not know how to work, who is lazy."

ref: An ax is ready to cut the trees down at their roots. Any tree that doesn't produce good fruit will be chopped down and thrown into a fire.
text: Pajar poŋ teen siira a wun chɔɔ tiinii; ki tiik nba kur ki loon lɔɔnŋana, bi sa chɔɔre ki lur muu ni.
translated: The bird that was sitting on the tree branch has flown away; it is not on the ground, but high up in the sky.

ref: If I use the power of Beelzebul to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? Your followers are the ones who will judge you.
text: Ki li-i tee ki Beelsebul teenin yikoo a mii nyint narinbiit niib ni, ki yimm muni, ŋmee mun chab i niib a bii nyint narinbiiti? I mɔŋ niib maŋ toonae want nan i ki mɔk mɔnii kaa.
translated: "Who is the one who brought Beelzebub's three-legged stool here and left it in the middle of the village square, who is the one who did this?" I do not know who it might be, but I will find out soon.
